name: chrX_Xq28_MECP2_duplication_SD
description: Xq28 recurrent ~0.5 Mb duplication encompassing MECP2 and flanking SDs (~5 kb at 99% identity), the most common dosage-sensitive locus on chrX. MECP2 duplication syndrome is one of the most frequent recurrent X-linked CNV disorders (1:150 X-linked ID males). BPs cluster in low-copy repeats just telomeric to int22h-3 (cross-ref Xq28_recurrent.json).

architecture.internal_structure: Direct ~5-10 kb SDs flanking MECP2; non-recurrent BPs in surrounding LCRs also common.

related_diseases: MECP2 duplication syndrome (X-linked severe ID, infections, autism)
Rett syndrome (MECP2 point mutations - inverse phenotype)
